sections_cited: Section 54 of the Land Acquisition Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No. D-8393 of 2017 [Abdul Aziz Naeemi V. Province of Sindh and another] Date Order with signature of Judge(s) Before: Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi Date of hearing and Order: 31.03.2026 Mr. Manzoor Hussain Khoso, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Ali Safdar Depar, A.A.G. ********** O R D E R Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. – Petitioner Abdul Aziz Naeemi has filed these Constitution Petitions under Article 199 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973, seeking following relief:- To, call R & P of the Reference No. 01/2014 from the learned Court of IIND ADJ Thatta and set aside impugned order dated 12.08.2015 and direct the respondent to pay the compensation of acquired land viz. Survey No. 285/1, 285/2, and 285/4 of. 2. Earlier, in C.P No. D-2374/2010, the petitioner challenged the compensation for land acquired for the Right Bank Outfall Drain (RBOD) project in Deh Gujjo, Taluka Mirpur Sakro, District Thatta. 3. It appears that Section 4 notification was also issued, and a Section 18 reference (Land Reference No.1/2014) was filed before the District Judge, Thatta. However, the acquiring department valued the land at Rs.70,000/- per acre plus statutory additions, while the petitioner claimed higher compensation, citing loss of cultivation. 4. The learned A.A.G submitted that instead of filing an appeal under Section 54 of the Land Acquisition Act, the petitioner filed this Constitution Petition, which pending since 2017. 5. when confronted this legal position of the case, the petitioner sought disposal of this petition with permission to file Appeal against the decision of the learned District Judge, let the petitioner file a proper appeal under the Act, however that is subject to all just exceptions as provided under the law. 6.

However, the acquiring department valued the land at Rs.70,000/- per acre plus statutory additions, while the petitioner claimed higher compensation, citing loss of cultivation. 4. The learned A.A.G submitted that instead of filing an appeal under Section 54 of the Land Acquisition Act, the petitioner filed this Constitution Petition, which pending since 2017. 5. when confronted this legal position of the case, the petitioner sought disposal of this petition with permission to file Appeal against the decision of the learned District Judge, let the petitioner file a proper appeal under the Act, however that is subject to all just exceptions as provided under the law. 6. Without touching the merits of the case, with the consent of the parties, this petition is disposed of with all pending applications accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE
